data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn mk(a: A): OptionA / {} { OptionA.some(a) }
---
mk : fn(A) -> OptionA
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn ok_mk(a: A): OptionA / {} { OptionA.some(a) }
---
ok_mk : fn(A) -> OptionA
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badp1(a: OptionA, b: A): A / {} { match a { some(x) => b, .none => b } }
---
ERROR: constructor patterns must start with `.`
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badp2(a: OptionA, b: A): A / {} { match a { some => b, .none => b } }
---
ERROR: variant pattern `some` must be written `.some`
==========
data Nat { .zero, .succ(Nat) }
data Option[A] { .some(A), .none }
fn sub1(x: Nat): Option[Nat] / {} { match x { .zero => Option.none, .succ(let x) => Option.some(x) } }
---
sub1 : fn(Nat) -> Option[Nat]
==========
data Nat { .zero, .succ(Nat) }
data Option[A] { .some(A), .none }
fn get_or_zero(o: Option[Nat], z: Nat): Nat / {} { match o { .some(x) => x, .none => z } }
---
get_or_zero : fn(Option[Nat], Nat) -> Nat
==========
data Option[A] { .some(A), .none }
fn ok_nil(u: Unit): Unit / {} { let x = Option.none; u }
---
ok_nil : fn(Unit) -> Unit
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn not(x: Bool): Bool / {} { match x { .true => Bool.false, .false => Bool.true } }
---
not : fn(Bool) -> Bool
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn not(x: Bool): Bool / {} { match x { .true => Bool.false(), .false => Bool.true() } }
---
ERROR: which is not a function
